headline: OpenAI's Real-Time API Hits Technical Friction as Privacy Scrutiny Intensifies
body: OpenAI is confronting infrastructure challenges with its WebRTC implementation for real-time voice interactions while facing regulatory pressure from Canadian privacy watchdogs over data handling practices. The company simultaneously launched GPT-Realtime-2 and ended support for fine-tuning APIs, signaling a shift in its product strategy. For healthcare AI implementations, these developments create immediate concerns around real-time clinical communication tools, patient data compliance in cross-border deployments, and the stability of OpenAI's API roadmap for production workflows. Organizations evaluating OpenAI for telehealth, clinical documentation, or real-time decision support need clarity on infrastructure reliability and regulatory alignment before committing to implementations. The combination of technical friction and privacy investigations suggests healthcare vendors should stress-test alternative architectures and diversify their AI infrastructure dependencies.
